#+TITLE: Auto-card pipeline — operational contract (Path A)
#+STARTUP: content

The contract the "missing card" automation follows. The *how-to-implement* and the
*FINISH vs PROPOSE decision* live in [[file:../.claude/commands/add-card.md][.claude/commands/add-card.md]] — this file is only the
*operational* layer: how a run consumes an issue, what it emits, and the guardrails it
must never cross. Implements Path A (recompile + redeploy); runtime data-cards (Path B,
serde the IR) are deferred.

* Status (2026-06-16): issue-filing only

The *front half* is live: the goldfish web frontend (~dd-goldfish.html~) detects
unimplemented cards and files pre-filled ~missing-card~ issues — no API key, no
secret. The *back half* (claude-code-action auto-implementer) is DORMANT pending an
Anthropic API account; ~.github/workflows/auto-card.yml~ is parked on
~workflow_dispatch~ so filed issues don't spawn failing runs. Everything below
describes the back half for when it's switched on. Until then, issues are worked by
hand following [[file:../.claude/commands/add-card.md][add-card.md]].

* Scope

Automate the cards the goldfish flags as missing, *only* when they can be implemented
faithfully with primitives that exist today. Correctness is never traded for throughput.
The trust boundary is the branch: the bot writes only to ~quarantine~; ~main~ is reached
only by a human-reviewed promotion PR.

* Input: a ~missing-card~ issue

One card per issue, labeled ~missing-card~. Body carries (filled from the Scryfall MCP at
file time):
- card name (exact)
- Oracle text, mana cost, types, P/T
- source decklist URL that surfaced it
- failure mode: ~✗ not in catalog~ vs ~~ in catalog but inert~ (from ~warn_unimplemented_cards~)

Dedup by card name: skip if an open issue or PR already covers it.

* The run (claude-code-action)

1. Check out the ~quarantine~ branch; create ~card/<slug>~ from it.
2. Follow [[file:../.claude/commands/add-card.md][add-card.md]] end to end, *including its FINISH vs PROPOSE gate*.
3. Classify, then emit one of the two outputs below.

* Output A — FINISH (card is Tier-1)

- Implement per the playbook; ~cargo build && cargo test~ must be green.
- Open a **ready** PR, base ~quarantine~, label ~auto-card~.
- CI green is required to merge. Auto-merge into ~quarantine~ is permitted on green
  (the branch is untrusted by design); auto-merge into ~main~ is *never* permitted.

* Output B — PROPOSE (card is "scary" / Tier-2 / uncertain)

- Do **not** write card code.
- Open a **draft** PR, base ~quarantine~, label ~needs-primitive~, body =
  clause-by-clause analysis: which clause blocks, which of the four primitive categories
  it needs, the generality the primitive should live at, and the blocking signal word.
- Leave it as a draft — it is a human design decision, not a merge candidate.

* Invariants (never cross these)

- Never push to, or open a PR against, ~main~. ~main~ receives only reviewed
  ~quarantine -> main~ promotion PRs.
- Never auto-merge into ~main~. ~quarantine~ auto-merge only on green CI.
- Never relax the no-hacks ratchet "because it's quarantine." Quarantine lowers the bar
  for *landing in staging*, not for *correctness*. A hack that builds + passes goldfish is
  exactly the silent-corruption case, and batch promotion review is *less* likely to catch
  it than a focused PR review.
- If ~cargo test~ fails and can only be made green by being more specific than the Oracle
  text → downgrade to PROPOSE. Never force a pass.
- One card per issue / per PR; per-card commits preserved (revert a bad card cleanly).

* Trigger

- Live path: ~on: issues~ (labeled ~missing-card~) — fires in seconds.
- Fallback only: a scheduled sweep to catch dropped events. GitHub ~schedule:~ cron is
  5-min-minimum and frequently delayed/dropped, so it is the garbage-collector, never the
  primary trigger.

* Promotion

Periodically a human opens a ~quarantine -> main~ PR — this is where real review
concentrates (batch, not per-card). Preserve per-card commits so a bad card is a clean
~git revert~, not surgery on the shared ~all_cards()~ vec.

* Prerequisites (yours to provision — not done by this spec)

- ~ANTHROPIC_API_KEY~ repo secret for claude-code-action.
- A ~quarantine~ branch with protection: CI green required before merge.
- The workflow YAML (~.github/workflows/auto-card.yml~) — Path A step 3, not yet written.
- Deploy + the goldfish wasm frontend (Path A step 4) — downstream; the frontend
  (mirroring the ~pilegen~ cdylib) does not exist yet.
